page: Microsoft Azure D4nlds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Dnldv6 server family. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 architecture with 4 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The server is equipped with 236 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, though it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators.

From a resource density perspective, the D4nlds_v6 is classified as a low-memory, network-optimized configuration. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions it as a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without the overhead of large memory allocations. The inclusion of 236 GB of local disk space provides a direct storage tier for temporary files or caching, reducing the dependency on external storage resources for basic operations.

This hardware profile is suited for network-optimized tasks, lightweight web serving, distributed compute nodes, and basic database operations that fit within the 8.0 GB memory limit. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing performance for compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D4nlds_v6 is a network-optimized virtual machine from the Dnldv6 family, built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of memory, establishing a 2.0 GB per core ratio that suits compute-heavy, low-memory workloads. The instance includes 236 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it lacks integrated GPUs and complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This configuration offers a cost-efficient balance for resource-constrained applications, prioritizing dedicated processing power over memory capacity. It is designed for network-optimized tasks, distributed compute nodes, caching layers, and lightweight web serving.
